COURT NOTES MOTION IS UNOPPOSED. HOWEVER, AS REQUESTED BY DEFENDANT THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBIT A TO WILLIAMS DECLARATION WHERE IN PLAINTIFF FILED A NON -OPPOSITION TO THE SAME PREVIOUSLY FILED MOTION. SEE EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(D), PEOPLE V. CAVANNA (1989) 214 CAL. APP. 3D 1054, 1058 AND LAZZARONE V. BANK OF AMERICA (1986) 181 CAL. APP.3D 581, 590. MOTION IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-611499 WITH CASE CGC-23-609858
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT REESE BENTON's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-611499 WITH CASE CGC-23-609858.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Court notes motion is unopposed. However, as requested by defendant the Court takes judicial notice of Exhibit A to Williams declaration where in Plaintiff filed a non -opposition to the same previously filed motion. See Evidence Code section 452(d), People v. Cavanna (1989) 214 Cal. App. 3d 1054, 1058 and Lazzarone v. Bank of America (1986) 181 Cal. App.3d 581, 590. Motion is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Barbara A. Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/BAZ)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-611499 WITH CASE CGC-23-609858
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
KENNETH FRUCHT, ESQ., GEONETTA & FRUCHT, LLP (510) 254-3777 Attorney for Plaintiff UNITED CALIFORNIA ACCESS & SECURITY
Steven N. Williams, Esq., STEVEN WILLIAMS LAW, PC. 415-697-1509 swillliams@stevenwilliamslaw.com Attorney for Defendant Reese Benton

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
